page: Amazon Web Services m8i.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2104.0 and top-tier text processing capabilities scoring 2919.0. Single-threaded gzip compression reaches 33.95 GB/sec, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression performs at 14.33 GB/sec. Multi-core operations, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance (8640.95) and Geekbench multi-core score (8228.0), fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests show 640.03 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 69.77 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads.

From a resource density perspective, the m8i.2xlarge offers a balanced hardware profile suitable for general-purpose workloads. The inclusion of DDR5 memory and dedicated Intel Xeon cores provides stable execution without the overhead of shared-core architectures, though the lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment. This instance is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and light LLM inference tasks utilizing small to medium models.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance powered by an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Utilizing the Nitro hypervisor, it features 4 physical cores, a 480 MB L3 cache, and 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. It does not include local storage or GPUs, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, particularly in text processing and single-threaded compression, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance. This instance is optimized for balanced workloads, including web serving, database operations, software compilation, and small-scale machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 vCPUs, an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor, 32.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix extension support for diverse workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor at 3.9 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 advanced instruction sets
Delivers 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and database operations
